preamble: The phonetic transformation from the near-open front unrounded vowel /æ/ to the close front unrounded vowel /i/ involves a significant raising of the tongue body within the front vowel space. This shift is a form of vowel raising, where a lower front vowel becomes a higher front vowel.

examples.note: Second-person masculine singular independent pronoun 'you' in some Dhofari Arabic dialects, specifically Salalah, Taqah, and il-Hafa. The short vowel /a/ has an allophone [æ] which can be raised to [i].

sources: Rubin, A. D. (2016). Coastal Dhofari Arabic: A Sketch Grammar. Brill.
